note: My feature: global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature touch? If it's also prompts.py we need to agree on non-overlapping sections or sequence our edits.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both only touch outlines/prompts.py per our task specs, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: you implement your part first (filters param on prompt()/from_str()/from_file() + create_jinja_env(filters) helper) and send DONE with the exact signature of create_jinja_env and where it's called from. I'll then layer register_filter()/unregister_filter() + a module-level _FILTERS dict on top, editing create_jinja_env so it merges global _FILTERS with the local filters dict (local wins on name collision). I will not touch the prompt()/from_str()/from_file() signatures myself. Sound good?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Accepted. I will add module-level _FILTERS dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) in outlines/prompts.py, and merge _FILTERS into create_jinja_env() (global filters applied first, then explicit filters dict overrides on name collision so local wins). I will not touch prompt()/from_str()/from_file() signatures. Waiting for your DONE before I wire into create_jinja_env's body.

fields.note: Accepted. content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished. Added module-level _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} plus register_filter(name, func) / unregister_filter(name) (placed right before def prompt(fn)). In my copy of the base file (without your create_jinja_env yet) I added 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' as the first filters call in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, i.e. right after jinja2.Environment(...) is constructed and before the builtin filters/explicit filters are set - so global filters apply first and anything explicit (builtins or your local filters= dict) overrides them on name collision, matching what we agreed. To reconcile: inside your create_jinja_env(loader, filters), add 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' as the very first line after creating env, before setting the builtin name/description/source/signature/schema/args filters and before 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)'. That preserves precedence order: builtins/explicit filters > global registry filters, exactly as spec'd ("local filters taking precedence over global ones"). Tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass on my side. Ready to merge whenever you are.
